title: hieracium murorum subsp. pseudoincrassans
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. pseudoincrassans, also known as wall hawk's beard, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family native to Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its sesquiterpene lactone content. The medicinal properties of Hieracium murorum subsp. pseudoincrassans have been reported to offer health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and hepatoprotective effects. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from its leaves and flowers, which can be dried and used in teas, infusions, or as a tincture for medicinal purposes.
